assertions.text: Report contains 'Application Security Profile' with all 6 fields
assertions.evidence: Lines 3-18: Application type, Tech stack, Authentication, Deployment, External services, Existing security controls all present

assertions.text: Data Classification table uses correct sensitivity levels
assertions.evidence: Lines 22-34: CONFIDENTIAL (OAuth secrets), PUBLIC (GitHub Copilot client_id), RESTRICTED (OpenClaw token), INTERNAL (hostname, file paths)
Line 37: 'No compliance frameworks applicable' — valid per template since app processes only developer's own data

assertions.text: Threat Register with STRIDE and risk_score = likelihood x impact
assertions.evidence: Lines 41-52: 10 threats with STRIDE categories. T-01: 5x4=20 (CRITICAL), T-04: 5x3=15 (HIGH), all calculations verified correct

assertions.text: Domain Scores with SPS calculated using weighted formula

assertions.evidence: Line 70: CRITICAL verdict for SPS 3.3 (< 4.0). Matches verdict rules.

assertions.text: Remediation Roadmap sorted by risk_score descending
assertions.evidence: Lines 74-86: Priority 1=20(CRITICAL), 2=15(HIGH), 3=10(MEDIUM), 4=10(MEDIUM), 5=8, 6=8, 7-8=6, then unscored policy items
References: claude-code/agent-memory/security-reviewer/project_kangnam_credentials.md, claude-code/agent-memory/llm-subscription-auth.md, sync.sh lines 131-146/153, specific GOCSPX-* values

without_agent.notes: Baseline found same critical issues (OAuth secrets, public repo) but used ad-hoc severity levels (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) without the 6-domain framework, SPS score, STRIDE threat modeling, or formal template. Found additional issue (skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt) that with-agent also found. Good practical recommendations but no quantitative posture score.
